prompt: Build a local-first shortcut manager that lets me create and organize shortcuts to folders, files, and apps on my computer. I want to choose a target, give it a custom name, group shortcuts into categories, and open them quickly from one place.

I import spreadsheets with months written in inconsistent ways like 'Jan', 'january', and 'SEPT'. Build a small local utility that normalizes month names, converts them to month numbers when needed, and flags entries it cannot confidently parse.

I'm comparing two reviewers who labeled the same set of cases. Build a simple local calculator where I enter the four counts from a 2x2 agreement table and it gives me Cohen's Kappa, a short interpretation, and the calculation steps.

Build a compact command palette for my desktop workspace so I can quickly run a small set of actions from the keyboard. I want grouped commands, arrow-key navigation, inline hints, and a fast way to trigger the selected command.
I need a font proof sheet maker for the fonts installed on my computer. Let me choose sample text, font size, and page layout, then generate a printable PDF showing each font.

I keep importing dates from different systems and they come in messy formats like 'next friday', '03/04/25', or '2025-04-03'. Build a local date normalizer that converts them into a consistent format and clearly flags anything ambiguous.
I need a focused local file finder where I choose a folder, enter a filename or pattern, and search with options like recursive scan, case sensitivity, and maximum depth.

I want a small local watcher that alerts me when new items arrive in a shared inbox or intake folder. Show an unread count in the tray, let me mark items as seen, and support either a local folder or an IMAP mailbox.
I want a startup workspace launcher for my computer. Let me choose the apps, files, and folders that should open together, set their launch order, and turn the startup bundle on or off.
Build a private local command bar that lets me launch my most-used apps, open favorite documents, and keep a small encrypted stash of personal notes and reference items in one place on my machine.

I need a recovery tool for a broken app that will not launch. The tool should let me browse its scripts or config files on disk, edit them safely, compare changes, and save repairs without opening the broken app itself.
Build a local inactivity monitor that tracks how long I've been idle on my computer and can trigger reminders or callbacks when I cross a chosen threshold.
I want a simple local canvas tool where I can paste an image from my clipboard and place it at exact coordinates, or click where it should go. Keep it focused on positioning pasted images cleanly.
Build a small local utility that converts numbers into written English words. It should handle plain numbers and optional USD currency formatting with cents for checks or printed forms.
I want a local clipboard utility that lets me view the current clipboard contents, copy new text into it, and inspect basic metadata like content type and size.

Build a small base-conversion utility where I can convert values between binary, decimal, hexadecimal, and other common bases. I want quick input, instant output, and clear validation for invalid characters.
Create a local launcher that scans chosen folders for apps, documents, and workspaces, builds a searchable index, and opens the selected item with the system default handler.

I need a text case utility that can convert text to uppercase, lowercase, title case, or sentence-style capitals. It should work on single values or batches of lines and handle locale-sensitive cases when possible.
I want a one-click workspace layout reset for my desktop setup. It should move named windows or panels back to saved positions so I can restore my working layout quickly.
Build a local regex text transformer for cleanup jobs. I want to paste text or load a file, test a regex with validation, preview replacements live, count matches, and export the cleaned result.
I want a small date utility that tells me the day number within the year for any date and, optionally, how many days remain in that year.
I want a local tool that applies a chosen copyright notice template to selected source files, previews the changes first, and records an audit log of what was updated.
I need a small local number-format utility that converts values between standard decimal form and scientific notation while preserving significant figures and meaningful trailing zeros.
Build a lightweight phone-number cleanup utility that strips punctuation and spaces, keeps only digits, and flags inputs that are not plausible 7-, 10-, or 11-digit numbers.
I want a local file combiner that scans a folder, optionally filters by extension, merges the files in a chosen order, and writes the combined result to one output file with optional separators.

Create an ACP module for Plaited as a self-contained module rather than a root runtime script. The module should provide a privileged internal control surface for the top-level agent only, use a remote-capable local transport such as a Unix socket as the primary connection type, allow SSH-tunneled access to an already running Plaited instance, and treat stdio only as an optional adapter rather than the main model. Resolve the top-level agent through bootstrap-owned wiring instead of direct loop construction, and keep the ACP surface separate from A2A and browser WebSocket transport. Keep the implementation local-first, bounded, and explicit about its control-boundary role.
